audit_status.source_base: Hebrew Exodus / Shemot text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: First-pass draft reviewed for Moses' signs, speech objection, Aaron, return to Egypt, firstborn language, difficult circumcision episode, and initial belief/worship.

verses.restored: Moses answered and said, But look, they will not trust me and will not listen to my voice, for they will say, YHWH has not appeared to you.
YHWH said to him, What is that in your hand? He said, A staff.
He said, Throw it to the ground. He threw it to the ground, and it became a serpent, and Moses fled from before it.
YHWH said to Moses, Stretch out your hand and grasp it by the tail. He stretched out his hand and held it, and it became a staff in his palm.
So that they may trust that YHWH, Divine of their fathers, Divine of Abraham, Divine of Isaac, and Divine of Jacob, has appeared to you.
YHWH said further to him, Please put your hand into your bosom. He put his hand into his bosom, and brought it out, and look, his hand was afflicted like snow.
He said, Return your hand to your bosom. He returned his hand to his bosom and brought it out from his bosom, and look, it returned like his flesh.
And it shall be, if they do not trust you or listen to voice of first sign, they may trust voice of later sign.

And it shall be, if they do not trust even these two signs or listen to your voice, then take from waters of Nile and pour on dry land; the waters you take from Nile will become blood/life on dry land.
Moses said to YHWH, Please, my Lord, I am not a man of words, neither yesterday nor before nor since you spoke to your servant, for I am heavy of mouth and heavy of tongue.
YHWH said to him, Who set a mouth for human? Or who makes mute, deaf, seeing, or blind? Is it not I, YHWH?
Now go, and I myself will be with your mouth and teach you what you shall speak.
He said, Please, my Lord, send by hand of whom you will send.

The anger of YHWH burned against Moses, and he said, Is there not Aaron your brother, the Levite? I know that speaking, he can speak. And also look, he is coming out to meet you; he will see you and rejoice in his heart.
You shall speak to him and put the words in his mouth. I myself will be with your mouth and with his mouth, and I will teach you what you shall do.
He shall speak for you to the people; he will be for you as mouth, and you will be for him as Divine.
And this staff you shall take in your hand, with which you shall do the signs.

Moses went and returned to Jethro his father-in-law and said to him, Please let me go and return to my brothers who are in Egypt and see whether they are still alive. Jethro said to Moses, Go in peace.
YHWH said to Moses in Midian, Go, return to Egypt, for all the men seeking your life are dead.
Moses took his wife and sons, set them on the donkey, and returned to land of Egypt; and Moses took staff of the Divine in his hand.
YHWH said to Moses, When you go to return to Egypt, see all the wonders that I put in your hand, and do them before Pharaoh; but I will strengthen/harden his heart, and he will not send the people.
You shall say to Pharaoh, Thus says YHWH: Israel is my son, my firstborn.
And I said to you, Send my son so he may serve/worship me; but you refused to send him. Look, I am about to kill your son, your firstborn.
And it happened on the way, at the lodging place, that YHWH met him and sought to put him to death.
Zipporah took a flint, cut the foreskin of her son, touched his feet, and said, Surely a bridegroom of bloods you are to me.
And he let him alone. Then she said, Bridegroom of bloods, because of circumcisions.
YHWH said to Aaron, Go to meet Moses in wilderness. He went and met him at mountain of Divine and kissed him.
Moses told Aaron all words of YHWH with which he had sent him, and all signs he had commanded him.
Moses and Aaron went and gathered all elders of children of Israel.
Aaron spoke all words that YHWH had spoken to Moses, and he did the signs before eyes of the people.
The people trusted, and they heard that YHWH had visited the children of Israel and had seen their affliction; they bowed low and worshiped.

translation_notes: Moses' speech objection is preserved without mocking disability; Divine response is presence with mouth and Aaron as support.
Hardening language begins here and requires careful tracking across Exodus; rendered strengthen/harden where Hebrew hazaq/kaved/qashah distinctions need later audit.
Israel firstborn language frames the confrontation with Pharaoh's firstborn policy from chapter 1.
Zipporah's circumcision episode is difficult and anciently obscure; preserve ambiguity and avoid overconfident interpretation.
safety_note: Exodus 4 contains dangerous and obscure death/circumcision material. Treat it as source-preserved, not as a ritual template. Do not use speech/disability material to shame Moses; the narrative provides Aaron as accommodation and partnership.
